sections.paragraph:
μοιχεία, ας, ἡ (μοιχεύω; Andocides et al.; Heraclit. Sto. 69 p. 89, 15; PMich 148 I, 8; LXX; TestSol 1:2 D; GrBar; Philo; Jos., Ant. 16, 340 al.; apolog.; τὴν μ. τῆς παρθένου καὶ τοῦ Πανθήρα Orig., C. Cels. I, 32, 6) adultery lit. Hm 4, 1, 5 and 9; ApcPt 9:24. W. other sins (Hos 4:2; Theoph. Ant. 2, 34 [p. 186, 9]) Gal 5:19 v.l.; 1 Cl 30:1; 2 Cl 6:4; 20:1; D 5:1; Hm 8:3. Pl. (as in D 5:1 above) denoting separate acts (Pla., Rep. 4, 443a, Leg. 8, 839a; PTebt 276, 16 πορνεῖαι καὶ μοιχεῖαι; GrBar; Philo, Spec. Leg. 2, 13; Jos., Bell. 5, 402; Just., D. 93, 1; Mel., P. 50, 362.—B-D-F §142; W-S. §27, 4d; s. Rob. 408) adulterous acts Mt 15:19=Mk 7:22 (Did., Gen. 226, 21; Theoph. Ant. 1, 14 [p. 92, 6]; in a list of vices also Plut., Mor. 1050d); cp. D 3:3; AcPl Ha 1, 12. Cp. καταλαμβάνειν ἐπὶ μοιχείᾳ catch in the act of adultery J 8:3 (Plut., Mor. 291f; on the ‘adulterous woman’ s. REisler, ZNW 22, 1923, 305–7; KBornhäuser, NKZ 37, 1926, 353–63; EBishop, JTS 35, ’34, 40–45; JJeremias, ZNW 43, ’51, 148f: a temptation story, cp. Mk 12:13–17; TManson, ZNW 44, ’52/53, 255f; FSchilling, ATR 37, ’55, 91–106; UBecker, Jesus u. die Ehebrecherin ’63; JDerrett, Law in the NT, ’70, 156–87; JMcDonald, NTS 41, ’95, 415–27). Also εὑρίσκειν ἐν μ. Hm 4, 1, 4.—B. 1456; RAC IV 666–77. DELG s.v. μοιχός. M-M. TW. Sv.

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Heraclit. Sto. Heraclit. Sto. = Heraclitus the Stoic, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

PMich PMich = Michigan Papyri—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

GrBar GrBar = Greek Baruch (Apocalypsis Baruchi Graece)—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

apolog. apolog. = apologist(s)

Orig Orig , var. works, II–III a.d.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Hm Hm = Mandates

ApcPt ApcPt = Apocalypse of Peter—List 1

Theoph. Ant. Theoph. Ant. = Theophilus Antiochenus, II a.d.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

W-S. W-S. = GWiner, Grammatik des neutestamentlichen Sprachidioms, ed. PSchmiedel—List 6

Rob. Rob. = ATRobertson, A Grammar of the Greek NT in the Light of Historical Research—List 6

Did. Did. = Didymus Caecus (the Blind), commentator on OT books, IV a.d.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

AcPl Ha AcPl Ha = Acts of Paul, PHamb—List 1

ZNW ZNW = Zeitschrift für die ntl. Wissenschaft—List 6

NKZ NKZ = Neue Kirchliche Zeitschrift—List 6

JTS JTS = Journal of Theological Studies—List 6

ATR ATR = Anglican Theological Review—List 6

NT NT = New Testament

NTS NTS = New Testament Studies—List 6

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

RAC RAC = Reallexikon für Antike u. Christentum—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Sv Sv (at the end of entries)=HSieben, Voces—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 656
